summary.interpretation: Frozen embedding receivers recover part of the semantic-anchor held-out packet signal with clean source-destroying controls, but none of the tested BGE/MiniLM variants clears the strict bidirectional gate. This weakens the hypothesis that a generic frozen text embedding alone can replace the explicit public semantic-anchor lexicon; the live next branch is a learned receiver or a better public ontology calibration layer, not another fixed-basis packet tweak.
